emails.body:
LCDR Maddox,

Red Hat Government is hosting a virtual briefing on October 15 covering open source strategies for edge compute and automation in contested environments. Sessions include:

- OpenShift at the tactical edge: disconnected operations and intermittent connectivity patterns
- Ansible Automation Platform for rapid baseline enforcement across heterogeneous fleets
- Case study: USMC logistics node modernization using RHEL for Edge
- Supply chain security: SBOM generation and SLSA compliance in air-gapped networks

The briefing runs 1000-1200 ET. Registration link below. CLE credit available for acquisition professionals.

https://events.redhat.example/gov-edge-2026

Respectfully,
Red Hat Government Team
government@redhat.example

LCDR Maddox,

Reminder: our "Ansible for Regulated Environments" workshop is this Thursday, October 10, 1300-1600 ET. This hands-on lab walks through:

- STIG baseline automation with Ansible Lockdown
- Evidence generation for RMF/ATO packages
- GitOps workflows for continuous compliance monitoring
- Integration with ACAS and SCAP tooling

Seats are limited to 30 participants. If you registered previously, your spot is confirmed. If not, register here:

https://events.redhat.example/ansible-regulated-2026

Questions? Reply to this email or contact our federal solutions architects at fed-architects@redhat.example.

Red Hat Government Team
government@redhat.example
